summary: Test suite covers all core requirements (manifest parsing, license checking, allow/deny lists, unknown status, error handling, mocking) with well-organized fixtures and clear test structure. However, rigor is weak—tests focus on happy path with minimal edge case coverage. Missing: empty manifests, invalid JSON, malformed configs, case sensitivity issues, requirements.txt version operators (>=, ^), special characters in package names, and loose assertions (substring matching instead of exact output validation). Design is sound with good fixture isolation and readability, though assertions could be more precise and test 14 is somewhat redundant. Would catch major regressions but miss subtle issues.
